rationale: This is a canonical example of the Houben-Hoesch reaction, featuring the acylation of a highly activated, electron-rich phenol (resorcinol) with a simple nitrile (acetonitrile). The reaction is catalyzed by a Lewis acid (zinc chloride) and a Brønsted acid (HCl), leading to an aryl ketone after hydrolysis of the intermediate ketimine. This example clearly illustrates the key transformation: the formation of a C(aryl)-C(acyl) bond from an arene and a nitrile.
collision_avoidance_note: This example uses a simple, highly activated phenol (resorcinol) and a simple aliphatic nitrile (acetonitrile). It avoids using benzene or other less activated arenes that might overlap with Friedel-Crafts acylation. It also avoids complex nitriles or arenes that could introduce ambiguity or overlap with other named reactions involving aromatic systems.
